title: Useful generic functions that can be used in DARApipelines
text: DARAutils (part DARAtools) R package offers generic function useful DARA pipelines.

previous_headings: Installation and updating > From RIVM GitLab
title: Useful generic functions that can be used in DARApipelines
text: install DARAutils GitLab, first install graph package running: can now download latest version DARAutils via internal Gitlab environment using remotes package: Please visit Wiki find instructions install older versions DARAutils.
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("BiocManager") } BiocManager::install("graph") if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("remotes") } remotes::install_gitlab("dara/DARAutils@main", host = "https://gitlab.rivm.nl", build = FALSE)

previous_headings: Installation and updating > From RIVM GitLab
title: Useful generic functions that can be used in DARApipelines
code: remotes::install_gitlab("dara/DARAutils@develop", host = "https://gitlab.rivm.nl", build = FALSE)

title: Useful generic functions that can be used in DARApipelines
text: install DARAutils GitHub, first install graph package running: can now download latest version DARAutils via GitHub using remotes package:
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("BiocManager") } BiocManager::install("graph") if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("remotes") } remotes::install_github("rivm-syso/DARAutils@main", build = FALSE")

title: Useful generic functions that can be used in DARApipelines
text: r library(DARAutils) load DARAutils package, part DARAtools framework. packages within DARAtools : DARAgit, git functionality. DARAvis, data visualisation RIVM-house style. DARAgeo, loading geographical data. DARApipeline, running EPI data pipeline.

what: Converts a date to ISOweek format (yyyy-Wxx) — ISOweek
title: Converts a date to ISOweek format (yyyy-Wxx) — ISOweek
text: function returns year week year given date according ISO 8601. Mimics ISOweek function ISOweek package, uses lubridate functions internally.

title: Converts a date to ISOweek format (yyyy-Wxx) — ISOweek
code: ISOweek(date)

title: Converts a date to ISOweek format (yyyy-Wxx) — ISOweek
text: date date datetime: date (vector dates) want convert ISOweek.

title: Converts a date to ISOweek format (yyyy-Wxx) — ISOweek
text: character form yyyy-Wxx, e.g. "2022-W52" "2021-W52" "2020-W12".

title: Converts a date to ISOweek format (yyyy-Wxx) — ISOweek
Cut and label function — cut_and_label
text: Cuts vector breaks labels levels.

title: Cut and label function — cut_and_label
code: cut_and_label(   x,   breaks,   right = TRUE,   sep = " t/m ",   extend_inf = TRUE,   break_steps = 1 )

title: Cut and label function — cut_and_label
text: x integer vector cut labeled. breaks Vector labels. right Logical, right hand side breaks included labels? sep Character, separator used labels break points. extend_inf Logical, breaks extended -Inf Inf? break_steps numeric, exact break steps (e.g. 1, .1, .01)

title: Cut and label function — cut_and_label
text: rounded value x digits digits.

title: Cut and label function — cut_and_label
code: cut_and_label(c(1, 5, 10), breaks = c(2, 5), right = TRUE, sep = "-", extend_inf = TRUE) #> [1] <=2 3-5 6+  #> Levels: <=2 3-5 6+ cut_and_label(c(1, 5, 10), breaks = c(2, 10), right = FALSE, sep = " t/m ", extend_inf = FALSE) #> [1] <NA>    2 t/m 9 <NA>    #> Levels: 2 t/m 9 cut_and_label(c(.1, .2, .4, .7, .9), breaks = c(.3, 0.7), break_steps = .1) #> [1] <=0.3       <=0.3       0.4 t/m 0.7 0.4 t/m 0.7 0.8+        #> Levels: <=0.3 0.4 t/m 0.7 0.8+  dplyr::starwars |> dplyr::mutate(height_category = cut_and_label(height, breaks= c(100, 200), right = FALSE, sep = "-", extend_inf = TRUE)) #> # A tibble: 87 × 15 #>    name     height  mass hair_color skin_color eye_color birth_year sex   gender #>    <chr>     <int> <dbl> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>          <dbl> <chr> <chr>  #>  1 Luke Sk…    172    77 blond      fair       blue            19   male  mascu… #>  2 C-3PO       167    75 NA         gold       yellow         112   none  mascu… #>  3 R2-D2        96    32 NA         white, bl… red             33   none  mascu… #>  4 Darth V…    202   136 none       white      yellow          41.9 male  mascu… #>  5 Leia Or…    150    49 brown      light      brown           19   fema… femin… #>  6 Owen La…    178   120 brown, gr… light      blue            52   male  mascu… #>  7 Beru Wh…    165    75 brown      light      blue            47   fema… femin… #>  8 R5-D4        97    32 NA         white, red red             NA   none  mascu… #>  9 Biggs D…    183    84 black      light      brown           24   male  mascu… #> 10 Obi-Wan…    182    77 auburn, w… fair       blue-gray       57   male  mascu… #> # ℹ 77 more rows #> # ℹ 6 more variables: homeworld <chr>, species <chr>, films <list>, #> #   vehicles <list>, starships <list>, height_category <fct>

what: Import rds file(s) from a specified path — import_rds_to_object
title: Import rds file(s) from a specified path — import_rds_to_object
text: Import rds files specified path optionally date. default, rds files today imported.

title: Import rds file(s) from a specified path — import_rds_to_object
code: import_rds_to_object(   path,   date = today(),   skip_modification_check = FALSE,   pattern = ".",   recursive = FALSE )

title: Import rds file(s) from a specified path — import_rds_to_object
text: path Path rds files located. date Date rds files contain file name. skip_modification_check Skip using date find file. Default: FALSE. pattern Optional character string defining pattern filename. recursive Logical. listing recurse directories?

title: Import rds file(s) from a specified path — import_rds_to_object
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ import_rds_to_object(path = "./example", date = as.Date("2000-01-01")) import_rds_to_object(path = "./example", skip_modification_check = TRUE) } # }

what: Union of a set of named lists/vectors in which the last occurrence for each entry is returned. — inheriting_merge
title: Union of a set of named lists/vectors in which the last occurrence for each entry is returned. — inheriting_merge
text: Takes lists vectors returns named union. named entry occurs last entry returned. Unnamed entries never returned. function can useful defining set default argument varying list arguments need overwrite defaults. object class first entry used return value.

title: Union of a set of named lists/vectors in which the last occurrence for each entry is returned. — inheriting_merge
code: inheriting_merge(..., call = parent.frame())

title: Union of a set of named lists/vectors in which the last occurrence for each entry is returned. — inheriting_merge
text: ... Named lists/vectors. call calling function used warning tracing. Default = parent.frame().

title: Union of a set of named lists/vectors in which the last occurrence for each entry is returned. — inheriting_merge
text: object class first input containing inherited union inputs.

title: Union of a set of named lists/vectors in which the last occurrence for each entry is returned. — inheriting_merge
code: default_args <- list(a = 1, b = "something") overwriting_args <- list(a = 2, c = "foo") overwriting_args2 <- list(a = 3, b = "else")  inheriting_merge() #> list() inheriting_merge(default_args) #> $a #> [1] 1 #>  #> $b #> [1] "something" #>  inheriting_merge(default_args, overwriting_args) #> $a #> [1] 2 #>  #> $b #> [1] "something" #>  #> $c #> [1] "foo" #>  inheriting_merge(default_args, overwriting_args, overwriting_args2) #> $a #> [1] 3 #>  #> $b #> [1] "else" #>  #> $c #> [1] "foo" #>

what: Executes join function 'join_f' to data frames x and y — join_date_sequence
title: Executes join function 'join_f' to data frames x and y — join_date_sequence
text: Executes join function 'join_f' data frames x y, y population data. Y 'date_y' variable specifies moment population valid

title: Executes join function 'join_f' to data frames x and y — join_date_sequence
code: join_date_sequence(x, y, by, date_x, date_y, join_f = left_join, ...)

title: Executes join function 'join_f' to data frames x and y — join_date_sequence
text: x Data.frame / tibble, data joined. y Data.frame / tibble, population data. Character, extra column join x y (besides date columns), e.g. "Veiligheidsregio". NULL column. date_x Date vector, variable x. date_y Date vector, variable y (population). Becomes '' date. join_f Function, one : left_join, right_join, inner_join, full_join. ... Optional.

title: Executes join function 'join_f' to data frames x and y — join_date_sequence
text: Joint data.frame x y. Attention: column date_y added.

title: Executes join function 'join_f' to data frames x and y — join_date_sequence
code: x <- data.frame(   dates = seq.Date(     from = as.Date("2020-01-01"),     to = as.Date("2022-07-01"),     by = "6 months"   ) ) y <- tibble::tribble(   ~Date_poll, ~Population, ~Country,   as.Date("2020-01-01"), 17407585, "Netherlands",   as.Date("2021-01-01"), 17475415, "Netherlands",   as.Date("2022-01-01"), 17590672, "Netherlands" ) join_date_sequence(x = x, y = y, by = NULL, date_x = dates, date_y = Date_poll) #>        dates  Date_poll Population     Country #> 1 2020-01-01 2020-01-01   17407585 Netherlands #> 2 2020-07-01 2020-01-01   17407585 Netherlands #> 3 2021-01-01 2021-01-01   17475415 Netherlands #> 4 2021-07-01 2021-01-01   17475415 Netherlands #> 5 2022-01-01 2022-01-01   17590672 Netherlands #> 6 2022-07-01 2022-01-01   17590672 Netherlands

what: Turn off logging for the duration of the parent function's execution and restore — local_logger_sink

title: Send mail — send_mail
text: Sends automated e-mail. Can useful running pipeline, e.g. succes failure.

title: Send mail — send_mail
code: send_mail(   subject,   body,   recipients,   is.html = FALSE,   sender = "noreply@domain.nl",   attachments = NULL,   cc = NULL,   bcc = NULL )

title: Send mail — send_mail
text: subject Character. Subject email. body Character. Body email. recipients List characters. Mail addresses recipients. .html Boolean. content type email html? sender Character. Sender email. attachments Vector characters. Paths attachments. cc Character. CC mail addresses. bcc Character. BCC mail addresses.

title: Send mail — send_mail
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ send_mail(   subject = "Test mail",   body = "This is a test e-mail",   recipients = "hello[@]example.com" ) } # }

what: Converts the old-style config file to the new multiple config files — upgrade_config
title: Converts the old-style config file to the new multiple config files — upgrade_config
text: function upgrade singular old style config file new multi-file config files. new config files enables use functions pipeline_init(),  pipeline_run() pipeline_import_for().

title: Converts the old-style config file to the new multiple config files — upgrade_config
code: upgrade_config(   config_file_old = "config/config.yaml",   config_dir_new = "config" )

title: Converts the old-style config file to the new multiple config files — upgrade_config
text: config_file_old String. Location old config file. config_dir_new String. Location new config folder.

title: Converts the old-style config file to the new multiple config files — upgrade_config
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ upgrade_config() } # }

title: xlabel_weekyear — xlabel_weekyear
text: Given character vector ISOweeks, labels first ISOweek year year-number others without year-number, thus week- number.

title: xlabel_weekyear — xlabel_weekyear
code: xlabel_weekyear(isoweeks)

title: xlabel_weekyear — xlabel_weekyear
text: isoweeks character vector ISOweeks.

title: xlabel_weekyear — xlabel_weekyear
code:
isoweeks <- ISOweek(seq.Date(from = as.Date("2023-01-01"), to = as.Date("2023-02-01"), by = "week")) xlabel_weekyear(isoweeks = isoweeks) #> [1] "52
2022" "1
2023"  "2
"      "3
"      "4
"

title: DARAutils v0.6.0
text: Common mistake linters added linted whole package (#66). Changed CICD Rpackage setup. Now first scans known libpaths (including docker image), installing missing packages. results faster CICD-pipeline runtime. (#90). Moved citation file correct location.

title: DARAutils v0.1.2
text: Added script send_mail contains function send_mail. Added script import_rds_to_object contains function import_rds_to_object.

title: DARAutils v0.1.2
text: Added script generic_processing contains functions cut_and_label inheriting_merge. Added script date_processing contains functions ISOweek, join_date_sequence, xlabel_weekyear.

title: DARAutils v0.1.2
text: <<<<<<< HEAD - Downgraded dependency withr 3.0.0, problems caused R OOD library still 3.0.0 version (#25). Edited script logger, local_logger_sink function added script

title: DARAutils v0.1.0
text: Added function local_logger_sink, helps testing locally capture output logger temp file. Added function formatter_cli layout_generator_subject, custom functions logger. Added function xlabel_weekyear, labels first ISOweek year year-number others without year-number week-number. Added function join_date_sequence, executes dplyr join function join date sequences. Added function ISOweek, returns year week given date according ISO 860. Added function inheriting_merge, merges set named lists/vectors last occurrence entry returned. Added function cut_and_label, cuts vector breaks labels levels. Added magrittr’s pipe operator use example cut_and_label. Added function upgrade_config, converts old-style config file new multiple config files.
